title: Full Analysis of Fourteenth Amendment Equal Protection and Due Process Violations (2015-2025)
content:
# Full Analysis of Fourteenth Amendment Equal Protection and Due Process Violations (2015-2025)

## Washington & Montana Legal Matters - Elvis Nuno

The systematic targeting of Elvis Nuno involved extensive Fourteenth Amendment violations across both Equal Protection and Due Process guarantees.

## Timeline of Violations

| # | Date & Location | Government Action | Fourteenth Amendment Violation | Economic Damage |
|---|-----------------|-------------------|--------------------------------|------------------|
| 1 | 14 Mar 2016 - Edmonds, WA | $75,000 bail on fabricated DV order violation | Procedural Due Process violation | $20,000 |
| 2 | Nov 2017-Aug 2018 - Missoula, MT | Officer Smith multiple arrests; removed for impropriety | Equal Protection selective enforcement | $537,000 |
| 3 | 15 Aug 2018 - Missoula, MT | Warrantless home entry and arrest | Substantive Due Process - shocking to conscience | $138,000 |
| 4 | 2020 - Multi-jurisdictional | Witness intimidation threats | Procedural Due Process - coercive plea bargaining | $87,000 |
| 5 | Feb 2021 - Missoula, MT | Parents' eviction after civil rights complaints | Equal Protection - retaliatory enforcement | $76,000 |
| 6 | 2015-2025 - Multi-jurisdictional | Systematic coordination to manufacture charges | Class-of-One Equal Protection | $3.44 million |

## Legal Analysis

### A. Equal Protection Clause Violations

**Class-of-One Theory**: The Supreme Court recognizes individuals can claim equal protection when intentionally treated differently from others similarly situated with no rational basis (Village of Willowbrook v. Olech).

### B. Procedural Due Process Violations

**Fabricated Evidence**: The 2016 Seattle arrest exemplifies systematic procedural violations despite exculpatory findings.

### C. Substantive Due Process Violations

**Conscience Shocking Conduct**: The coordinated decade-long campaign meets the demanding standard for substantive due process violations.

## Resulting Damages

1. **Economic Damages**: $3.44 million in documented losses
2. **Constitutional Violations**: Each incident establishes independent Section 1983 liability
3. **Municipal Liability**: Under Monell, cities face institutional liability
